plan_units.learning_objective: Recognize that core physician traits directly map to innovation leadership traits, lowering the psychological barrier to seeing oneself as an innovator.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From seeing innovation as foreign to recognizing it as an extension of existing clinical strengths through direct trait overlap.
plan_units.rationale: This opening concept is foundational but works best as a gestalt comparison; its value is in seeing the overlap simultaneously rather than decomposing it.
source_refs: You do not need to learn
plan_units.title: Physicianship = Innovation Leadership

plan_units.learning_objective: Map the clinical method to design thinking so learners understand innovation as a familiar problem-solving workflow.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From perceiving design thinking as business jargon to recognizing it as the same reasoning pattern used in daily clinical care through workflow mapping.
plan_units.rationale: This is a core translation move for the lesson and has multiple explanatory beats, but the side-by-side flowchart is most effective when kept whole as a direct comparison.
source_refs: you are performing the first step
plan_units.title: The Bridge: Diagnosis = Design Thinking

plan_units.learning_objective: Identify intrapreneurship as a structured, replicable approach rather than a vague personality trait.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From viewing intrapreneurship as vague to understanding it as a structured internal-change formula through guided reading and framing.
plan_units.rationale: The reading bridge introduces a key external framework explicitly required by the source and prepares learners for the intrapreneurship distinction that follows.
source_refs: a calculated approach to driving change from within
plan_units.title: Required Reading: The Intrapreneurship Formula

plan_units.learning_objective: Differentiate intrapreneurship from entrepreneurship and identify the hospital as the learner’s primary arena for innovation.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From conflating innovation with startup formation to understanding intrapreneurship as internal system redesign through contrast, constraint visualization, and organizational framing.
plan_units.rationale: This contrast is central to the module and naturally supports progressive disclosure: define the distinction, surface the internal constraints, then land the institutional-design mindset.
source_refs: improve it from the inside out
plan_units.title: Intrapreneurship vs. Entrepreneurship

plan_units.learning_objective: Explain why intrapreneurship is harder than entrepreneurship because change must occur within load-bearing institutional constraints.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From conflating innovation with startup formation to understanding intrapreneurship as internal system redesign through contrast, constraint visualization, and organizational framing.
plan_units.rationale: This interstitial supplies the tension beat by making visible the hospital constraints that complicate internal innovation.
source_refs: without destroying the existing, load-bearing institutional structures
plan_units.title: The Intrapreneur's Maze: Why Internal Innovation Is Harder

plan_units.learning_objective: Adopt the mindset that effective clinical innovation works within existing systems by understanding and navigating budgets, compliance, politics, and legacy infrastructure.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From conflating innovation with startup formation to understanding intrapreneurship as internal system redesign through contrast, constraint visualization, and organizational framing.
plan_units.rationale: This resolve interstitial lands the practical implication of the contrast and frames the course purpose in actionable terms.
source_refs: legacy IT systems, compliance rules, and operational budgets
redesign the room without breaking the building
navigating the constraints, politics, and resources
plan_units.title: Redesign the Room Without Breaking the Building

plan_units.learning_objective: Distinguish a raw idea from an actionable opportunity by using feasibility, viability, and desirability as the vetting standard.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From casually generating solutions to rigorously framing actionable opportunities through definition, first-principles thinking, and worked reframing.
plan_units.rationale: This is one of the highest-weight concepts in the lesson, with clear progression from definition to reasoning method to concrete example; it warrants a full cluster.
source_refs: An idea is simply a thought
rigorously vetted for feasibility, viability, and desirability
stop generating ideas, and start engineering actionable opportunities
plan_units.title: Idea vs. Opportunity

plan_units.learning_objective: Use first-principles thinking to break a broken workflow into fundamental truths instead of iterating on inherited analogies.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From casually generating solutions to rigorously framing actionable opportunities through definition, first-principles thinking, and worked reframing.
plan_units.rationale: This interstitial deepens the opportunity concept by introducing the reasoning method needed to move beyond superficial solutioning.
source_refs: Breaking it down to fundamental truths
Building a new system from the ground up
do not just iterate on "how we have always done it"
plan_units.title: The Deconstruction: First Principles Thinking

plan_units.learning_objective: Analyze a superficial solution statement and reframe it into a root-cause-based opportunity statement.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From casually generating solutions to rigorously framing actionable opportunities through definition, first-principles thinking, and worked reframing.
plan_units.rationale: This interstitial exemplifies the abstract distinction with a concrete med-surg discharge case, supporting transfer into learner practice.
source_refs: buy iPads for all the nurses
The Opportunity: To design a standardized, mobile-friendly discharge workflow
plan_units.title: Worked Example: Bad Idea to Good Opportunity

plan_units.learning_objective: Commit to problem framing over premature solution attachment as the standard for clinical intrapreneurship.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From casually generating solutions to rigorously framing actionable opportunities through definition, first-principles thinking, and worked reframing.
plan_units.rationale: This resolve unit consolidates the lesson’s core stance and supports the later knowledge check on problem-vs-solution attachment.
source_refs: fall in love with the problem
overly attached to their pre-determined solution
ignore the actual systemic root cause
plan_units.title: Opportunity Framing Means Falling in Love with the Problem

plan_units.learning_objective: Synthesize the module’s three takeaways: clinicians are already equipped to innovate, intrapreneurship is the arena, and opportunity framing is the goal.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From discrete concepts to an integrated clinician-intrapreneur mindset through concise recap and forward transition.
plan_units.rationale: This is a classic synthesis/resolve slide whose value is the simultaneous whole, so it should remain dense and unchunked.
plan_units.title: Part 3 Summary and Transition

plan_units.learning_objective: Demonstrate recall and understanding of the module’s core distinctions: idea vs. opportunity, clinical method vs. design thinking, intrapreneurship, problem-love, and lead domino logic.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From exposure to initial verification through targeted recall and comprehension checks.
plan_units.rationale: The knowledge check is explicitly included in the source as end-of-part assessment and should remain a single assessment unit.
source_refs: vetted for feasibility, viability, and desirability
balance your clinical vision with the realities of the organizational environment
fall in love with the problem
plan_units.title: Part 3 Knowledge Check

research_goals.pedagogical_intent: Learner may benefit from primary-source grounding for Sandra Lam’s intrapreneurship framework so the workbook can quote and contextualize the formula accurately.
Learner may need a sourced, organization-facing definition set distinguishing entrepreneurship from intrapreneurship for workbook reinforcement and faculty facilitation notes.

depth_delta.deferred_depth: A fuller side-by-side explanation of how History maps to Empathize, Diagnosis maps to Define, and Treatment planning maps to Ideate/Prototype, with room for reflective note-taking on learners’ own workflows.
depth_delta.retained_on_slide: The high-level side-by-side flow comparison and core mapping labels.

exercises.answer_key_source_ref: When you take a patient's history, you are performing the first step of Design Thinking: **Empathize**.
exercises.prompt_intent: Have learners explain the design-thinking equivalent of familiar clinical steps in their own words.

sections.narrative_intent: Slow down the translation so learners can internalize that innovation is not alien to clinical practice but structurally familiar.
sections.title: Clinical Method to Design Thinking Translation Guide

depth_delta.deferred_depth: A workbook spread that expands on internal constraints such as legacy IT, compliance, hospital politics, and operating budgets, with prompts for identifying which constraints exist in the learner’s own setting.
depth_delta.retained_on_slide: The core contrast between entrepreneurship and intrapreneurship plus the blueprint metaphor.

exercises.answer_key_source_ref: Label on the left: *"Entrepreneur: Building on a blank canvas."*
exercises.prompt_intent: Ask learners to sort real hospital constraints into categories and describe how each changes implementation strategy.

exercises.answer_key_source_ref: master the art of internal stakeholder alignment
exercises.prompt_intent: Help learners explain why internal stakeholder alignment matters for scale inside a hospital.

sections.narrative_intent: Make the implicit organizational complexity explicit so learners see intrapreneurship as disciplined redesign within a living system.
sections.title: Navigating the Intrapreneur's Maze

depth_delta.deferred_depth: Expanded criteria and guided worksheets for converting vague solution ideas into opportunity statements using root-cause framing, feasibility, viability, and desirability lenses.
depth_delta.retained_on_slide: The headline distinction between idea and opportunity.

exercises.answer_key_source_ref: An **Opportunity** is an idea that has been rigorously vetted for feasibility, viability, and desirability
exercises.prompt_intent: Guide learners to rewrite a superficial idea into a problem-framed opportunity statement.

exercises.answer_key_source_ref: It jumps straight to a solution (iPads) without defining the actual problem.
exercises.prompt_intent: Have learners identify where a statement jumps prematurely to solutioning rather than root cause.
Prompt learners to draft an opportunity statement from their own department’s broken workflow.

sections.narrative_intent: Provide the deliberate practice space that the slides should not carry, so learners can work through rigorous problem framing in depth.
sections.title: From Idea to Opportunity Workbook Lab

depth_delta.deferred_depth: Answer rationales and reflective prompts that explain why distractors are wrong and how each concept connects back to real clinical innovation decisions.

exercises.answer_key_source_ref: Why C is correct: An idea (like "We should use AI to write notes") has a value of zero on its own.
exercises.prompt_intent: Ask learners to justify the correct answer for each knowledge-check item using module language.

sections.narrative_intent: Turn the assessment from a simple checkpoint into a retrieval-and-explanation tool that strengthens retention.
sections.title: Knowledge Check Review and Reflection

lesson_plan.lesson_summary: Part 3 reframes physicians as natural innovators, maps clinical diagnosis onto design thinking, situates learners in intrapreneurship rather than entrepreneurship, and then sharpens the crucial distinction between a raw idea and a vetted opportunity. The clustering plan prioritizes two concept-dense areas: the intrapreneurship contrast and the idea-to-opportunity progression. Summary and knowledge check remain dense singletons because their pedagogical value lies in integrated recap and assessment rather than decomposition.
